title: The One with the Metaphorical Tunnel

jokes.text: ...but these cartons are so flinging, flanging hard to open!
jokes.setup: Infomercial actor describing difficulty opening milk cartons
jokes.punchline: Made-up curse words 'flinging, flanging' as euphemisms
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemistic wordplay that sounds like profanity but isn't. Economy is good but setup is generic infomercial parody.

jokes.text: -Intrigued? -You're flinging, flanging right I am!
jokes.setup: Callback to the made-up curse words from earlier
jokes.punchline: Using the euphemism in a different context for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that maintains the running gag but doesn't elevate it significantly.

jokes.text: It's official. There are no good movies. So let's go to a bad one, and make out.
jokes.setup: Complaining about lack of good movies
jokes.punchline: Practical solution that reveals romantic motivation
jokes.explanation: Nice structure with unexpected turn to practical romance. Good economy of language.

jokes.text: Perhaps you'd like me to turn so you can bunny-bump against my back?
jokes.setup: Awkward sexual suggestion
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic term 'bunny-bump' for sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Creative euphemism but setup feels forced. Mild cringe factor helps.

jokes.text: I'll tell you, I do enjoy guilt... but it wasn't me.
jokes.setup: Being accused of forgetting to deliver a message
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of enjoying guilt before denying responsibility
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor with perfect timing. The honesty about enjoying guilt is both funny and revealing.

jokes.text: Okay, it was me! -How was it you? -Well, it was all so crazy, you know?
jokes.setup: Quick confession followed by question about how it happened
jokes.punchline: Beginning of absurd explanation
jokes.explanation: Sets up for absurd explanation. Character-specific bumbling behavior.

jokes.text: Chandler was in the closet, counting to 10... and I hadn't found a place to hide yet.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he forgot to deliver message
jokes.punchline: Reveals they were playing hide and seek as adults
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Joey's childlike nature causing adult consequences. Absurd but believable for him.

jokes.text: I meant to tell you, and I wrote it all down on my hand. See? All of it.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to show he was responsible
jokes.punchline: Writing important info on hand (which presumably washed off or smudged)
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup. Very Joey-appropriate method that backfired predictably.

jokes.text: See? Now, this is why I keep notepads everywhere. Yep. And that's why we don't invite you to play.
jokes.setup: Smug comment about being organized with notepads
jokes.punchline: Immediate put-down revealing they exclude him from games
jokes.explanation: Good structure with immediate comeuppance for smugness. Sharp dialogue rhythm.

jokes.text: She only talks to agents. -What a sad little life she must lead.
jokes.setup: Casting director only talks to agents
jokes.punchline: Pitying comment about her social limitations
jokes.explanation: Nice observational humor with good delivery. Economical and character-appropriate sympathy.

jokes.text: Hi, this is Caitlin from Phoebe Buffay's office.
jokes.setup: Phoebe impersonating an assistant
jokes.punchline: Creating fake professional identity for herself
jokes.explanation: Very Phoebe to create this elaborate deception. Good character consistency with her unconventional approach.

jokes.text: So your husband leaves and burns down the apartment. The world doesn't stop.
jokes.setup: Phoebe improvising as an agent
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning increasingly dramatic personal disasters
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and very Phoebe-like casual mention of dramatic events. Good absurdist humor.

jokes.text: Get the woman a pad! Get the woman a pad! A pad! A pad!
jokes.setup: Phoebe desperately needs to write something down during phone call
jokes.punchline: Frantic shouting for writing materials
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with repetitive escalation. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Oh, now you want a pad.
jokes.setup: Reference to earlier notepad comment
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the ironic timing of needing notes
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect timing. Great economy and satisfying ironic payoff.

jokes.text: He carries it everywhere, like a security blanket. But with ski boots and a kicky beret.
jokes.setup: Explaining Ben's attachment to Barbie
jokes.punchline: Specific fashion details of the doll
jokes.explanation: Nice specific details that make the visual funnier. 'Kicky beret' is a good descriptive choice.

jokes.text: ...in show business.
jokes.setup: Ross worried about what Ben might grow up to be
jokes.punchline: Revelation that he's worried about show business, not sexuality
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts expectations. Perfect character moment for Ross - more worried about career than orientation.

jokes.text: Has anybody seen my left boob?
jokes.setup: Monica looking for something she's lost
jokes.punchline: It's a prosthetic breast (she's a chef who lost it)
jokes.explanation: Good shock value with economical delivery. Context makes it work well.

jokes.text: Here it is. What are you doing? I'm sorry. It just felt nice.
jokes.setup: Rachel found the prosthetic breast
jokes.punchline: Admission that she was touching/feeling it
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor with physical comedy element. Rachel's curiosity is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I only got one arm. You should do stuff for me. Get me a sweater.
jokes.setup: Monica's arm is in a sling
jokes.punchline: Immediately taking advantage to get waited on
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's opportunistic nature. Escalates quickly to demands.

jokes.text: And that's bad... because you hate chicken piccata?
jokes.setup: Chandler complaining about Janice sharing food
jokes.punchline: Rachel missing the point about relationship intimacy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Rachel doesn't understand Chandler's real issue with intimacy.

jokes.text: You didn't want to share your tomatoes. Tomatoes are very important to you.
jokes.setup: Continuing to miss Chandler's point
jokes.punchline: Making it about his attachment to tomatoes specifically
jokes.explanation: Escalates the misunderstanding absurdly. Rachel's complete misreading is funny and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I've never been through the tunnel myself because... you're not allowed through with more than one girl in the car.
jokes.setup: Joey offering relationship advice about commitment tunnel
jokes.punchline: Reveals his dating lifestyle prevents him from experiencing commitment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey line - tries to help but reveals his own player lifestyle. Great character consistency and clever metaphor use.

jokes.text: If I'm staring down the barrel of a gun... I'm gonna be pretty much peeing every which way.
jokes.setup: Joey's advice about facing fears with gun metaphor
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical/neurotic response about fear response
jokes.explanation: Great Chandler response - neurotic and practical. Good structure with unexpected but logical conclusion.

jokes.text: Sure, that's what it's there for. Emergencies and pretend agents.
jokes.setup: Joey asking to use the phone
jokes.punchline: Gunther's dry comment about Phoebe's agent act
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gunther delivery - dry observation with good economy. Nice callback to ongoing plot.

jokes.text: Went through a tunnel. Unbelievable.
jokes.setup: Overhearing Phoebe's phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Joey thinks she literally went through a tunnel during the call
jokes.explanation: Good Joey moment - takes metaphor literally. Perfect character consistency with his simple interpretation.

jokes.text: Are you a puppy!
jokes.setup: Chandler giving Janice a drawer
jokes.punchline: Janice's over-the-top baby talk reaction
jokes.explanation: Very Janice behavior - over-the-top and slightly cringey. Character-specific but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Well, I gotta buy a vowel... because, oh, my God!
jokes.setup: Janice overwhelmed by Chandler's gesture
jokes.punchline: Wheel of Fortune reference for being speechless
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits character but feels a bit forced. Functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: Who would've thought that someday... Chandler Bing would buy me a drawer?
jokes.setup: Janice reflecting on the significance
jokes.punchline: Treating a drawer like a major romantic milestone
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Janice's tendency to over-dramatize small gestures.

jokes.text: I don't think we need to because you're tripping me out right now!
jokes.setup: Chandler suggesting trip with her parents
jokes.punchline: Janice overwhelmed by his sudden commitment enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how Chandler's over-commitment is backfiring.

jokes.text: And I wasn't even that hungry.
jokes.setup: Janice listing complaints about Chandler's behavior
jokes.punchline: Random addition about not being hungry for pasta
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist touch - adds random complaint that reveals her mindset during the evening.

jokes.text: Don't go! I've scared you! I've said too much! I'm hopeless and awkward and desperate for love!
jokes.setup: Chandler realizing he's driven Janice away
jokes.punchline: Completely accurate but damaging self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler moment - self-aware but self-destructive. Good escalating desperation.

jokes.text: Oh, God. Welcome to our side of the tunnel.
jokes.setup: Chandler now being the needy one in relationship
jokes.punchline: Monica welcoming him to the typically female side
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with role reversal. Great economy and insight about relationship dynamics.

jokes.text: We save the real stuff for the truly terminal cases.
jokes.setup: Chandler complaining about low-fat ice cream
jokes.punchline: Rachel comparing relationship problems to medical triage
jokes.explanation: Dark humor comparing relationship issues to terminal illness. Good medical metaphor.

jokes.text: You're not terminal. We just need some damage control.
jokes.setup: Continuing the medical metaphor
jokes.punchline: Reassuring Chandler he's not hopeless
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to medical metaphor. Continues the supportive but clinical tone.

jokes.text: Oh, honey, you're not a total loser.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking if he's going to lose Janice
jokes.punchline: Backhanded reassurance with 'total' qualifier
jokes.explanation: Good structure with backhanded compliment. The word 'total' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I said, 'So I'm not gonna lose her?'
jokes.setup: Rachel's non-answer to his question
jokes.punchline: Chandler having to repeat himself after awkward silence
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing the girls avoiding his direct question.

jokes.text: Look, Ben! It's a toy that protects U.S. oil interests overseas!
jokes.setup: Trying to interest Ben in G.I. Joe
jokes.punchline: Cynical description of military action figures
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with sharp political commentary. Very Susan-like subversive take on children's toys.

jokes.text: Yes, they called! You didn't get it! Okay? I mean, you didn't get it. I'm sorry. I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to break bad news gently
jokes.punchline: Completely failing at gentle delivery
jokes.explanation: Very Phoebe - means well but terrible at delivery. Good escalation of awkwardness.

jokes.text: Now you're sad and creepy.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to look okay after rejection
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt assessment of his expression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe honesty. Great economy with 'sad and creepy' description.

jokes.text: They said they'd never met an Italian actor with a worse Italian accent.
jokes.setup: Phoebe explaining Joey's rejection
jokes.punchline: Brutal specific criticism of Joey's heritage performance
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Joey can't even do his own ethnicity convincingly. Harsh but funny.

jokes.text: Are you being British?
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to act aloof
jokes.punchline: Janice picking up on his affected speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Good observation of Chandler's tendency to affect accents when nervous. Sharp economy.

jokes.text: Barley? What kind of party serves barley?
jokes.setup: Chandler's fake shopping for party supplies
jokes.punchline: Janice questioning the ridiculous party food choice
jokes.explanation: Good setup for absurd lie. Janice's practical question exposes the ridiculousness.

jokes.text: I'm sorry if my friends aren't as sophisticated as yours.
jokes.setup: Defending his fake barley party
jokes.punchline: Getting defensive about imaginary friends' sophistication
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Chandler digging deeper into his lie. Character-appropriate defensiveness.

jokes.text: Either you're seeing somebody behind my back... which would make you the world's biggest jerk... or you're pretending to see somebody... which makes you so pathetic I could cry right here!
jokes.setup: Janice figuring out Chandler's deception
jokes.punchline: Two equally damning interpretations of his behavior
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure presenting two terrible options. Good escalation and very Janice-like directness.

jokes.text: Can I be that guy?
jokes.setup: Janice pointing to random guy in store
jokes.punchline: Chandler's defeated acceptance of any identity
jokes.explanation: Great economy and perfect defeated Chandler response. Visual element adds to the humor.

jokes.text: They said that you 'weren't believable as a human being.'
jokes.setup: Phoebe delivering another harsh rejection
jokes.punchline: Existentially brutal casting feedback
jokes.explanation: Brutally funny rejection that goes beyond acting to basic humanity. Excellent quotable line.

jokes.text: The off-Broadway-play people said that you were 'pretty but dumb.' Oh, no wait. I'm sorry. That's 'pretty dumb.'
jokes.setup: Seemingly giving Joey a compliment about being pretty
jokes.punchline: Correcting to even harsher criticism
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with cruel twist. Great structure building hope then crushing it.

jokes.text: My God, Chandler, we said be 'aloof,' not 'a doof.'
jokes.setup: Chandler's disastrous attempt at acting aloof
jokes.punchline: Monica's pun on aloof/a doof
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect setup. Great economy and memorable pun.

jokes.text: Yes! And grumpy! What are you? Stop naming dwarfs!
jokes.setup: Giving Chandler advice to be sleepy and grumpy
jokes.punchline: Monica realizing they're naming Snow White dwarfs
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and recognition of accidental pattern. Nice pop culture awareness.

jokes.text: He's so lucky. If Janice were a guy... she'd be sleeping with somebody else by now.
jokes.setup: Chandler getting back together with Janice
jokes.punchline: Gender-based observation about relationship tolerance
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about gender differences in relationship patience. Dark but insightful.

jokes.text: You used to dress up like a woman.
jokes.setup: Ross being judgmental about Ben's Barbie
jokes.punchline: Monica revealing Ross's own childhood cross-dressing
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling payback moment. Great economy and character-revealing moment.

jokes.text: You made us call you 'Bea'?
jokes.setup: Continuing revelation about Ross's childhood
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about his chosen feminine name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific embarrassing detail. 'Bea' is a funny old-fashioned choice.

jokes.text: Oh, please, God, let there be a song.
jokes.setup: Monica discovering there was a song component
jokes.punchline: Her gleeful anticipation of maximum embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Perfect sibling sadism. Monica's prayer for maximum embarrassment is very in-character.

jokes.text: I am Bea, I drink tea, Won't you dance around with me?
jokes.setup: The promised song about Ross's childhood persona
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously simple rhyming song
jokes.explanation: Maximum embarrassment achieved. Simple but effective childhood song that's memorable and cringe-worthy.

tmdb_overview: Phoebe poses as Joey's agent and has some success in getting him auditions. Ross panics when he sees his son with a Barbie doll. Chandler is advancing in his relationship with Janice.
